page: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific processing tasks. The m7i.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, alongside strong results in image and text processing. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 32.91 GB/sec. In database and memory operations, the instance achieves average tier results, with a Passmark database score of 15226.36 and Redis SET operations reaching 37.27 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions support top-tier text generation speeds of 413.98 tokens per second on small LLM models, and strong prompt processing speeds of 175.93 tokens per second on medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for workloads requiring modern DDR5 memory speeds and dedicated Intel Xeon compute without the overhead of local storage. The lack of bundled local NVMe storage or GPUs represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes 16 physical cores and a 105 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which contribute to strong performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core benchmarks, as well as high-throughput static web serving at 46.4 GB/sec. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It delivers average-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, while achieving top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for enterprise workloads and CPU-based inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.8xlarge features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 12 Gbps bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring DDR5 memory and Intel AMX acceleration for balanced compute and memory workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors at 3.2 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong performance in static web serving and small-model LLM inference
Suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and databases
